paper_title: CHANGES OF THERMOCLINE DEPTH AT THE SUMBA ISLAND OFFSHORE BASED ON PLANKTONIC FORAMINIFERAL ASSEMBLAGES AND ITS IMPLICATION TO EUTROPHICATION SINCE THE LAST DEGLACIATION (~18 ka BP): A PRELIMINARY STUDY

methods.description: Process for collecting sub-samples from gravity cores, preparing them using the swirling method, washing, drying, splitting, identifying, and quantitatively counting foraminifera specimens for analysis.

steps.description: A total of 250 sub-samples were collected and ~5 g from each sub-sample were prepared using the swirling method (without H 2 O 2 ) to disengage the foraminifera specimens from the mud.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect ~5g sub-samples from gravity cores

steps.description: ...~5 g from each sub-sample were prepared using the swirling method (without H 2 O 2 ) to disengage the foraminifera specimens from the mud.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment samples using swirling method (without H2O2) to disengage foraminifera
outputs: prepared sediment samples with disengaged foraminifera

steps.description: Each sample was washed on the top of 200 mesh (0.074 mm) sieve...
steps.searchable_summary: Wash prepared samples on a 200 mesh (0.074 mm) sieve

steps.description: ...and the residue was dried at the temperature of 60 o C for ~6 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry sieved residue at 60°C for ~6 hours

steps.description: Quantitative determination of foraminiferal taxa was preceded by splitting the prepared sample until ~300 specimens of foraminifera were present in each part.
steps.searchable_summary: Split dried sample until ~300 foraminifera specimens are present in each part
outputs: split samples with target specimen count (~300)

steps.description: All identified taxa in one part were counted...
steps.searchable_summary: Count all identified foraminifera taxa in one split part
inputs: foraminifera identification guides (Kenneth and Srinivasan 1983, Bolli and Saunders 1985)
outputs: raw counts of identified taxa from one split

steps.description: ...while new taxa detected in the remaining parts were counted as one specimen even after normalization.
steps.searchable_summary: Count new taxa detected in remaining split parts as one specimen
inputs: list of taxa identified in step 6
outputs: counts of new taxa from remaining splits

steps.description: The foraminiferal counts were normalized against the number of splits and the weight of the samples (Ardi et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize foraminiferal counts by number of splits and sample weight

methods.method_name: Radiocarbon Dating and Age Model Generation
methods.description: Process for obtaining radiocarbon ages from core samples, calibrating them to calendar ages using specific methods and datasets, and generating an age model for the core using the Clam package in R.

steps.description: Accelerator Mass Spectrometer-measured radiocarbon (AMS 14 C) ages were only available on five depth intervals of core ST08 (see Table 2) (Ardi, 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain AMS radiocarbon ages for specific depth intervals of core ST08
inputs: core ST08 samples from specified depth intervals

steps.description: The radiocarbon ages were converted to calibrated calendar and radiocarbon ages with High Probability Density Range (HPD) Method (Bronk Ramsey, 2009) (for 24-25 cm, 74-75 cm, and 104-105 cm depth intervals)... based on the MARINE13 calibration datasets (Reimer et al., 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate radiocarbon ages using HPD method and MARINE13 dataset for specified intervals

steps.description: ...and the intercept of radiocarbon age with calibration curve (Talma and Vogel, 1993) (for 166-167 cm and 235-236 cm depth intervals) based on the MARINE13 calibration datasets (Reimer et al., 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate radiocarbon ages using intercept method and MARINE13 dataset for specified intervals

steps.description: The age model for the core ST08 was generated using the Clam package (version 2.3.4) on R (Blaauw, 2010, 2020; R Core Team, 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Generate sediment core age model using Clam package (v2.3.4) in R

steps.description: Twenty runs were done...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform 20 runs of age model generation

steps.description: ...and a model with the lowest goodness-of-fit (-log) was chosen.
steps.searchable_summary: Select age model with the lowest goodness-of-fit (-log)

steps.description: This model employed linear interpolation, weighted average-based calendar age point estimates for depths, and the standard Gaussian distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure age model with linear interpolation, weighted average, and Gaussian distribution

steps.description: The core top sediment was assumed to be aged -66 Before Present (BP) (Present=1950 Anno Domini/AD, core ST08 was retrieved in 2016)...
steps.searchable_summary: Assume core top age is -66 BP (1950 AD)

steps.description: ...and the sedimentation rates between the aged intervals were assumed to be constant...
steps.searchable_summary: Assume constant sedimentation rates between dated intervals

steps.description: Relative age based on planktonic foraminiferal zonation (Bolli and Saunders, 1985) was also utilized to establish the chronological framework in this study.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize planktonic foraminiferal zonation for relative age dating

methods.description: Method to infer changes in the Depth of Thermocline (DOT) by calculating the relative abundances of specific planktonic foraminifera groups (thermocline dwellers vs. mixed layer dwellers) and using their ratio.

steps.description: The relative abundances of thermocline dwellers (i.e. Pulleniatina obliqueloculata, Neogloboquadrina spp. and Globorotalia spp.)...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and count thermocline dweller planktonic foraminifera taxa
inputs: identified and counted foraminifera taxa (from Foraminifera Sample Preparation and Counting method)
list of thermocline dweller taxa (Pulleniatina obliqueloculata, Neogloboquadrina spp., Globorotalia spp.)
outputs: counts of thermocline dweller taxa per subsample

steps.description: ...and mixed layer dwellers (i.e. Globigerinoides ruber, Globigerinoides trilobus, and other Globigerinoides taxa) planktonic foraminifera...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and count mixed layer dweller planktonic foraminifera taxa
inputs: identified and counted foraminifera taxa (from Foraminifera Sample Preparation and Counting method)
list of mixed layer dweller taxa (Globigerinoides ruber, Globigerinoides trilobus, other Globigerinoides taxa)
outputs: counts of mixed layer dweller taxa per subsample

steps.description: (ratio of the total number of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers against the total number of planktonic foraminifera in a subsample)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total count of thermocline dwellers per subsample
inputs: counts of individual thermocline dweller taxa per subsample
outputs: total count of thermocline dwellers per subsample

steps.description: (ratio of the total number of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers against the total number of planktonic foraminifera in a subsample)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total count of mixed layer dwellers per subsample
inputs: counts of individual mixed layer dweller taxa per subsample
outputs: total count of mixed layer dwellers per subsample

steps.description: (ratio of the total number of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers against the total number of planktonic foraminifera in a subsample)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total count of all planktonic foraminifera per subsample
inputs: normalized foraminiferal counts (from Foraminifera Sample Preparation and Counting method)
outputs: total count of planktonic foraminifera per subsample

steps.description: (ratio of the total number of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers against the total number of planktonic foraminifera in a subsample)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative abundance of thermocline dwellers (total thermocline dwellers / total planktonic foraminifera)
inputs: total count of thermocline dwellers per subsample
total count of planktonic foraminifera per subsample
outputs: relative abundance of thermocline dwellers per subsample

steps.description: (ratio of the total number of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers against the total number of planktonic foraminifera in a subsample)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative abundance of mixed layer dwellers (total mixed layer dwellers / total planktonic foraminifera)
inputs: total count of mixed layer dwellers per subsample
total count of planktonic foraminifera per subsample
outputs: relative abundance of mixed layer dwellers per subsample

steps.description: The shoaling (deepening) of the DOT was indicated by the increase...
steps.searchable_summary: Infer changes in Depth of Thermocline (DOT) based on relative abundances of thermocline/mixed layer dwellers
inputs: relative abundance of thermocline dwellers per subsample
relative abundance of mixed layer dwellers per subsample
established relationships from literature (Bé et al., 1977; Ravelo et al., 1990)
outputs: inferred changes in Depth of Thermocline (DOT) over time/depth
